memoryArtifacts.headline: Family of Herbert from Annals and Antiquities, vol. II, pg. 776-777
memoryArtifacts.description: Family of Herbert from Annuals and Antiquities, vol. II, pg. 776-777. However, it is noted, that Herbert of Vermandois (Herbertus Camerarius) was not the Count of Vermandois, but rather his uncle was, Herbert IV, Count of Vermandois. It is also noted that the son of Herbertus Camerarius, Henry FitzHerbert (Henry the Treasurer) is missing from the Annals and Antiquities, but his grandson Herbert (Herbert FitzHenry) and great grandson Herbert  (Herbert FitzHerbert) are included.

zh.lifeSummary: 當Jenkin ap Adam of Gwarinddu於大約1301年出生時，他的父親Adam FitzHerbert, Lord of Llanllowell28歲，母親Christian verch Gwarin Ddu25歲。他於大約1325年與Gwenlian verch Aron在英國結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和1個女兒的父母。他去世於1344年在威爾士蒙姆爾享年44歲，埋葬在英國。

nameMeanings.name: Jenkin
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Transferred use of the surname, which is derived from the medieval given name Jankin. This was a pet form of the boy's name Jan , with the diminutive suffix -kin. The modern given name is popular in Wales, where the surnames Jenkin and Jenkins are common.

nameMeanings.name: ap
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of any of the names beginning with this syllable.

variants.name: Alexander
meanings: From the Latin form of the Greek name Alexandros, from alexein ‘to defend’ + anēr ‘man, warrior’ (genitive andros). The name became extremely popular in the post-classical period, and was borne by several individuals in the New Testament and some early Christian saints. Its use as a common given name throughout Europe, however, derives largely from the fame of Alexander the Great , King of Macedon ( 356–323 bc ), around whom a large body of popular legend grew up in late antiquity, much of which came to be embodied in the medieval ‘Alexander romances’.

variants.name: Alan
meanings: Of Celtic origin and uncertain derivation (possibly a diminutive of a word meaning ‘rock’). It was introduced into England by Breton followers of William the Conqueror , most notably Alan , Earl of Brittany, who was rewarded for his services with vast estates in the newly conquered kingdom. In Britain the variants Allan and Allen are considerably less frequent, and generally represent transferred uses of surname forms, whereas in America all three forms of the name are approximately equally common. See also Alun .

variants.name: Albert
meanings: From an Old French name, Albert, of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from adal ‘noble’ + berht ‘bright, famous’. This was adopted by the Normans and introduced by them to England, displacing the Old English form Æþelbeorht. The name is popular in a variety of forms in Western Europe, and has been traditional in a number of European princely families. It was out of favour in England for centuries, however, and the revival of its popularity in the 19th century was largely in honour of Queen Victoria's consort, Prince Albert of Saxe-Coburg-Gotha .

nameMeanings.name: Adam
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.

nameMeanings.name: of
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of Oswald or any of the various other names beginning with Os-.

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

en.lifeSummary: When Jenkin ap Adam of Gwarinddu was born about 1301, his father, Adam FitzHerbert, Lord of Llanllowell, was 28 and his mother, Christian verch Gwarin Ddu, was 25. He married Gwenlian verch Aron about 1325, in England. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 1 daughter. He died in 1344, in Monmouthshire, Wales, at the age of 44, and was buried in England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Jenkin ap Adam of Gwarinddu est né environ 1301, son père, Adam FitzHerbert, Lord of Llanllowell, avait 28 ans et sa mère, Christian verch Gwarin Ddu, avait 25 ans. Il a épousé Gwenlian verch Aron environ 1325, à Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils et 1 fille. Il est décédé en 1344, à Monmouthshire, Galles, à l’âge de 44 ans, et a été enterré à Angleterre.

Er hat um 1325 in England Gwenlian verch Aron geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 2 Söhne und 1 Tochter. Er starb 1344 im Alter von 44 Jahren in Monmouthshire, Wales und wurde in England begraben.

ko.lifeSummary: Jenkin ap Adam of Gwarinddu(이)가 대략 1301년에 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Adam FitzHerbert, Lord of Llanllowell(은)는 28세였고, 그의 어머니 Christian verch Gwarin Ddu(은)는 25세였다. 그는 대략 1325년 영국에서 Gwenlian verch Aron(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1344년 웨일스 몬모우스실르에서 44세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Jenkin ap Adam of Gwarinddu nasceu aproximadamente 1301, seu pai, Adam FitzHerbert, Lord of Llanllowell, tinha 28 anos e sua mãe, Christian verch Gwarin Ddu, tinha 25 anos. Ele casou-se com Gwenlian verch Aron aproximadamente 1325, em Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhos e 1 filha. Ele faleceu em 1344, em Monmouthshire, Gales, com 44 anos, e foi sepultado em Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Jenkin ap Adam of Gwarinddu родился около 1301 г., его отцу по имени Adam FitzHerbert, Lord of Llanllowell было 28 лет, а матери, которую звали Christian verch Gwarin Ddu, 25. Он женился на женщине по имени Gwenlian verch Aron около 1325 г. в местности Англия. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына и 1 дочь. Он умер в 1344 г. в местности монмоутшир, Уэльс в возрасте 44 лет и был похоронен в местности Англия.
